annotations.label: Klein four-group

anchor.snippet: the Klein four-group is an abelian group with four elements

annotations.note: `IsKleinFour` is a mixin class for a group of cardinality four and exponent two.

annotations.label: Smallest non-cyclic group

anchor.snippet: the smallest group that is not cyclic

annotations.note: `IsKleinFour.not_isCyclic` shows it is not cyclic, but Mathlib has no statement asserting it is the smallest such group.

annotations.label: Only two groups of order 4

anchor.snippet: there is only one other group of order four

annotations.note: No classification theorem stating that every group of order 4 is either cyclic or Klein four is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group presentation

anchor.snippet: The Klein four-group is also defined by the group presentation

annotations.note: `PresentedGroup` provides the general machinery but Mathlib has no instance giving the explicit Klein four presentation.

annotations.label: Non-identity elements have order 2

anchor.snippet: All non- identity elements of the Klein group have order 2

annotations.note: `IsKleinFour.mul_self` proves `x * x = 1` for every element; the field `exponent_two` records the same fact.

annotations.label: Isomorphic to dihedral group of order 4

anchor.snippet: isomorphic to the dihedral group of order (cardinality) 4

annotations.note: The instance `IsKleinFour (DihedralGroup 2)` combined with `IsKleinFour.nonempty_mulEquiv` gives the isomorphism with any Klein four-group.

annotations.label: Only abelian dihedral group besides order 2

anchor.snippet: other than the group of order 2, it is the only dihedral group that is abelian

annotations.note: No theorem characterizing exactly which dihedral groups are abelian is recorded.

annotations.label: Isomorphic to direct sum Z2⊕Z2

anchor.snippet: The Klein four-group is also isomorphic to the direct sum

annotations.note: The instance `IsAddKleinFour (ZMod 2 × ZMod 2)` together with `IsAddKleinFour.nonempty_addEquiv` provides the isomorphism.

annotations.label: Elementary abelian 2-group / Boolean group

anchor.snippet: an example of an elementary abelian 2-group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general definition of elementary abelian p-group; only the rank-2 case via `IsKleinFour` is captured.

annotations.label: Symmetric difference on powerset

anchor.snippet: the group generated by the symmetric difference as the binary operation on the subsets of a powerset

annotations.note: No instance identifying the symmetric-difference group on a 2-element powerset with `IsKleinFour` is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Units mod 8

anchor.snippet: the set { 1, 3, 5, 7 } , with the operation being multiplication modulo 8

annotations.note: Mathlib does not record `(ZMod 8)ˣ` as a Klein four-group instance.

annotations.label: 2×2 real matrix representation

anchor.snippet: a representation as 2 × 2 real matrices with the operation being matrix multiplication

annotations.note: No explicit 2×2 matrix realization of the Klein four-group is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rubik's Cube 4 dots pattern

anchor.snippet: On a Rubik's Cube , the "4 dots" pattern can be made in three ways

annotations.note: Rubik's-cube formalizations are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetry group of rhombus/rectangle

anchor.snippet: the Klein four-group is the symmetry group of a rhombus and of rectangles that are not squares

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have a formal symmetry group of a rhombus or rectangle.

annotations.label: Three 3D symmetry groups

anchor.snippet: there are three different symmetry groups that are algebraically the Klein four-group

annotations.note: No three-dimensional point-group symmetry analysis identifying Klein four-group is formalized.

annotations.label: Automorphism group is S_3

anchor.snippet: the automorphism group of V is thus the group of permutations of these three elements

annotations.note: No theorem identifying `MulAut` of a Klein four-group with the symmetric group on 3 elements is present.

annotations.label: Permutation representation on four points

anchor.snippet: its permutation representation on four points

annotations.note: `alternatingGroup.kleinFour α` realizes the Klein four-group as the subgroup of `alternatingGroup α` generated by `(2,2)`-cycles when `α` has 4 elements.

annotations.label: Normal subgroup of A_4 and S_4

anchor.snippet: is a normal subgroup of the alternating group

annotations.note: Normality in `alternatingGroup α` (and characteristicity) is proved; the corresponding normality in `S₄` is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Transitive subgroup of S_4 as a Galois group

anchor.snippet: a transitive subgroup of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem identifying the Klein four-group as a transitive subgroup of `S₄` realized as a Galois group.

annotations.label: Kernel of S_4 → S_3

anchor.snippet: the kernel of a surjective group homomorphism from

annotations.note: No statement realizing the Klein four-group as the kernel of the surjection `S₄ → S₃` is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Other (non-normal) representations in S_4

anchor.snippet: Other representations within S 4 are

annotations.note: Alternate non-normal Klein four subgroups of `S₄` are not catalogued in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galois-theoretic role for quartics

anchor.snippet: the existence of the Klein four-group (and in particular, the permutation representation of it) explains the existence of the formula for calculating the roots of quartic equations

annotations.note: No Galois-theoretic treatment of quartic solvability involving the Klein four-group is formalized.

annotations.label: Additive substructure of rings of order 4

anchor.snippet: eight of the eleven rings with four elements have the Klein four-group as their additive substructure

annotations.note: Mathlib has no enumeration/classification of rings of order four.

annotations.label: Quotient of units of reals

anchor.snippet: The quotient group

annotations.note: No identification of a quotient of `ℝˣ` with the Klein four-group is recorded.

annotations.label: Quotient of units of split-complex numbers

anchor.snippet: the group of units of the split-complex number ring , when divided by its identity component, also results in the Klein four-group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal statement that the quotient of the split-complex unit group by its identity component is Klein four.

annotations.label: Diamond graph automorphism group

anchor.snippet: the simplest (in the sense of having the fewest entities) that admits the Klein four-group as its automorphism group is the diamond graph

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the diamond graph nor compute its automorphism group.

annotations.label: Four-group in twelve-tone technique

anchor.snippet: the four-group is the basic group of permutations in the twelve-tone technique

annotations.note: Twelve-tone music theory is not present in Mathlib.
